sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nottingham Forest have shown decent form in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, scoring consistently but struggling defensively with no clean sheets in 8 games. Chelsea, on the other hand, are in strong form with 4 wins in their last 5 matches, including a solid attacking output, though they suffered a recent loss to Newcastle.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, matches between Nottingham Forest and Chelsea have been competitive, with a 1-1 draw and a 3-2 win for Chelsea. Both teams have scored in 4 of their last 5 meetings, indicating tight games with goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Nottingham Forest rely heavily on Chris Wood (20 goals) for attacking threat, while Chelsea's Cole Palmer (15 goals, 8 assists) remains a standout. Chelsea are missing key forward Nicolas Jackson, which could impact their finishing, while Forest miss defender Murillo, potentially weakening their backline.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Nottingham Forest rank well defensively (6th for goals conceded) but struggle with possession (19th) and expected goals underperformance. Chelsea, positioned 5th in the league, have a balanced attack and defense, with strong final-third play, though they miss chances (as seen in recent games).

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Forest are likely to exploit set pieces (2nd in headers scored) and counterattacks (5th in fast breaks), while Chelsea may dominate possession and create big chances through players like Palmer. Chelsea's tendency to score late (highest % of goals in 76-90 min) could be decisive.
